rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Bank Levy Due To Improper Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, March 13, 2025, Line 1. DEFENDANT NICOLAS COLON's Motion To Vacate Bank Levy Due To Improper Service. Judgment debtor Nicolas Colon has filed a motion to vacate a bank levy due to improper service. CCP 1005(b) provides that moving papers must be served and filed 16 court days before hearing. CCP 1013(a) provides that service to an out-of-state address by regular mail extends the response period by 10 calendar days. Here, Colon filed and mailed the motion to a Washington address on February 18, 2025. This service was untimely for a March 13 hearing date. The court continues the matter for hearing to March 26, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 301 of this court. Judgment creditor Discover Bank may serve an opposition by nine court days prior to the hearing date. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: EDGAR LOPEZ VS. ANDREW CHEKENE ENTERPRISES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class And Paga Action Settlement Agreement And Release
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, March 13, 2025, Line 5. PLAINTIFF EDGAR LOPEZ, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED's Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class And Paga Action Settlement Agreement And Release. Hearing required. The release by settlement class members at para. 29 of the settlement agreement and release is extremely broad, including "all causes of action  arising during the Class Period that  could have been alleged in the Complaint." This is potentially as broad as the rules of joinder would allow. The court is not inclined to grant preliminary approval upon such a broad release. Apart from that concern, the court observes that the class notice proposed here is not as clear as model notices, such as the LA County Superior Court model document, LASC CIV 296. Further, class counsel should be prepared to provide, either at preliminary approval hearing or at final approval hearing, an estimated number of hours that Plaintiff devoted to this litigation. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: XIZHEN ZHU ET AL VS. KINGDOM OF DUMPLING LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, March 13, 2025, Line 2. PLAINTIFFS XIZHEN ZHU, AND YU LIN CHEN's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Complaint. Plaintiff's motion for leave to amend the complaint is denied. Plaintiff has not complied with the requirements of 3.1324. In particular, Plaintiff's motion is not accompanied by a declaration from counsel and does not state when the facts giving rise to the amended allegations were discovered or why the amendment could not have been earlier made. The motion also does not specify what allegations are proposed to be added or deleted by page and line number. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: AIMEE SILVER VS. GREYSTAR CALIFORNIA, INC., A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Matters Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, March 13, 2025, Line 4. DEFENDANTS GREYSTAR CALIFORNIA, INC., AND GDCV MARKET STREET 87, LLC's Motion To Deem Matters Admitted. Plaintiff Aimee Silver failed to serve timely responses to defendant Greystar California Inc.'s requests for admission. In addition her responses were unverified. (Recendez Dec. para. 7.) After Greystar filed its moving papers seeking an order establishing admissions and sanctions, Silver served verified responses. (Silver Dec. para. 3 & Exh. A.) Silver waived objections by failing to serve timely responses. CCP 2023.280(a). However, Greystar's motion for deemed admissions is denied because Silver has served substantially compliant, verified responses in advance of the hearing. CCP 2033.280(c). Although she recites objections, she provides substantive responses to each RFA. Greystar does not seek sanctions in its motion, but sanctions in this circumstance are mandatory, even where a party has substantially complied by the hearing date. (CCP 2033.280(c) ["It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion."].) The court awards sanctions in the amount of $100 payable by Silver to Greystar within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
